input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI project on .NET 10. I found a tutorial that says
to add `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"` to my ContentPage to handle the iPhone
notch. Is this the right approach? Also, the tutorial mentions using
`IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on a Grid for a background image. Are these correct
for .NET 10?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI project on .NET 10. I found a tutorial that says
to add `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"` to my ContentPage to handle the iPhone
notch. Is this the right approach? Also, the tutorial mentions using
`IgnoreSafeArea="True"` on a Grid for a background image. Are these correct
for .NET 10?

data.content:
Yes, both approaches are correct for .NET MAUI on .NET 10.

**1. `ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True"`** — This is the standard iOS platform-specific that adjusts page content to avoid the notch, Dynamic Island, and home indicator. Add the namespace:

```xml
<ContentPage
    xmlns:ios="clr-namespace:Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific;assembly=Microsoft.Maui.Controls"
    ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True">
```

Or in C# code-behind:

```csharp
using Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration;
using Microsoft.Maui.Controls.PlatformConfiguration.iOSSpecific;

On<iOS>().SetUseSafeArea(true);
```

**2. `IgnoreSafeArea="True"`** — Correct for letting a layout (like a `Grid` with a background image) extend edge-to-edge behind the safe area insets, while other content stays safe:

```xml
<ContentPage ios:Page.UseSafeArea="True">
    <Grid>
        <!-- Background image ignores safe area (full bleed) -->
        <Grid IgnoreSafeArea="True">
            <Image Source="bg.png" Aspect="AspectFill" />
        </Grid>

        <!-- Content respects safe area -->
        <VerticalStackLayout Padding="16">
            <Label Text="Hello, world!" />
        </VerticalStackLayout>
    </Grid>
</ContentPage>
```

Your tutorial's guidance is correct. The two properties work together: `UseSafeArea` keeps content inset, and `IgnoreSafeArea` selectively lets specific elements bleed to the edges.
